title: Chocolate on Silk
timeEstimate: Pro-lane within hours; cold-water blot only at home
stainChemistry: Chocolate is four-part: cocoa butter (fat) + milk casein (protein) + cocoa tannin (pigment) + sugar. On silk (protein fiber: fibroin) the protein step is the trap — enzymes that lift casein from cotton DIGEST silk equally. Hot water denatures both casein and silk simultaneously. Hydrogen peroxide yellows silk dyes. OxiClean weakens silk + darkens tannin. The home tools that work for chocolate on cotton damage silk in three different ways. Pro uses cold solvent (POG / petroleum ether) for fat + cold NSD for protein + acetic acid for tannin + sodium hydrosulfite for residual color — all at controlled dilution and timing.
whyThisWorks: A silk specialist with cold solvent + cold NSD + timed acid + reducing bleach can lift fresh chocolate without damaging fibroin. Home chemistry runs out of safe options fast. Cold-water blot is the entire safe home protocol; everything else risks fiber damage worse than the chocolate residue.
defaultAssumption: Silk + chocolate = pro within hours. Cold-water blot is the entire home protocol. Fresh, light chocolate may resolve fully at the pro; aged or dark chocolate often leaves a faint pigment ghost regardless of treatment.

spottingProtocol.technique: Ice bag harden + plastic card scrape
spottingProtocol.equipment: Ice in plastic bag, plastic card
spottingProtocol.instruction: Press a sealed bag of ice on the chocolate for 5 min to firm cocoa butter. Lift solid pieces with the edge of a plastic card. Don't scrape silk — flat-edge lift only. Most surface bulk comes off cleanly.
Flush cold water through the BACK of the stain over a white catch towel. Press, no rubbing. Removes residual cocoa + sugar.
Cold solvent dissolves cocoa butter without water-based agitation. Press cloth on stain, flip frequently. 2–3 passes.
Cold NSD handles casein without enzymes. Press only, no agitation.
Acetic acid 60 sec for residual tannin. If brown remains, sodium hydrosulfite 60 sec for oxidized residue. Flush cold between.

steps: 1. Ice + lift solid cocoa butter, no scraping into silk.
2. Cold water back-flush at first contact.
3. Cold POG / petroleum ether for cocoa butter.
4. Cold NSD sponge press for casein.
7. Final cold rinse, press flat to air-dry.
warnings: NEVER enzymes on silk — digest fibroin
NEVER hot water — denatures silk + sets casein
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes
NEVER OxiClean / alkaline — weakens silk + darkens tannin

homeSolutions.instruction: Chocolate on silk is the rare combination where every common home tool causes new damage. Enzymes (the cotton chocolate-protein-cleaning standard) DIGEST silk fibroin. Peroxide yellows silk dyes. OxiClean weakens silk AND darkens chocolate tannin. Home protocol = freeze and lift bulk, cold-water back-flush, then pro within hours. Fresh + careful gives the pro the best shot.

homeSolutions.agent: Ice + Lift Solid Chocolate — Don't Scrape Silk
homeSolutions.instruction: Press a zip-lock bag of ice on the chocolate for 5 minutes to firm the cocoa butter. Lift the solid pieces off with the FLAT edge of a plastic card. Don't scrape — silk fibroin tears under any sharp edge. Just lift the bulk. Refreeze if it softens.

homeSolutions.agent: Cold Water Back-Flush from the BACK
homeSolutions.instruction: Turn inside out. Place a white towel under the stain. Run COLD water through the BACK of the stain into the catch towel. Cold ONLY — heat denatures silk, sets casein, sets tannin all at once. Pour through, not on top — water rings silk.

homeSolutions.agent: Press Dry — DO NOT Apply Anything Else
homeSolutions.instruction: Press silk between two dry white towels. No wringing. Lay flat at room temperature, out of sun. Do NOT apply enzyme cleaner (digests silk), peroxide (yellows silk), OxiClean (weakens silk + darkens tannin), bleach, or detergent. The cotton chocolate-removal toolkit damages silk.

CALL FIRST — "Do you treat chocolate on silk with cold POG + sodium hydrosulfite?" If they don't understand, try another cleaner. Tell them: "Chocolate on silk (cocoa butter + milk protein + tannin), age [hours], freeze-and-lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No enzymes, no peroxide, no detergent applied."

steps: 2. Ice + lift solid bulk with flat edge of plastic card. No scraping.
3. Cold water back-flush from the reverse, into a catch towel.
4. Press dry between white towels — no other chemistry.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop after the cold-water back-flush. Anything else home-applied risks worse damage than the chocolate.
warnings: NEVER enzymes on silk — digests fibroin
NEVER hot water — denatures silk + sets casein
NEVER hydrogen peroxide on silk — yellows dyes
NEVER scrape silk — tears fibroin (flat-edge lift only)
Pour through, not on top — water rings

neverDo: Never enzymes on silk — digests fibroin
Never hot water on silk OR chocolate
Never scrape silk with a sharp edge
fiberSensitivities: Silk is protein fiber — same enzyme-digestion as casein
Alkaline weakens silk AND darkens tannin

materialWarnings: Pro within hours — home is freeze-and-blot only
Cotton chocolate-removal toolkit damages silk three ways
Flat-edge lift only — never scrape silk
Cold + press + pro is the only safe path

escalation.when: Always — silk + chocolate is pro-lane within hours, regardless of stain age.
escalation.whatToTell: "Chocolate on silk (cocoa butter + milk protein + tannin), age [hours / overnight], freeze-and-lift + cold-water back-flush ONLY at home. No enzymes, no peroxide, no detergent applied."
escalation.specialistType: professional dry cleaner specializing in silk (call ahead — confirm cold POG + sodium hydrosulfite spotting)

contextQuestions: How long ago — minutes, hours, or older?
Type — milk chocolate / dark chocolate / white?
Type of silk — charmeuse / chiffon / habotai / dupioni?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Dark chocolate has more cocoa tannin — harder. White chocolate has less pigment but more cocoa butter (fat). Pre-applied enzyme = silk fibroin likely already partially digested. Pre-applied peroxide = silk likely yellowed.
